revision.text:
[[ja:榛名神社 (沼田市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Haruna Shrine (榛名神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Numata, Gunma}} | location = {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|qid=Q666317|lt=Numata, Gunma|1=Numata, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Haruna Town 2851 | deity = {{ill|Haniyasu-hime|ja|埴山姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9285919}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}
{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Mitsumasha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumasha Nagare-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Latitude = 36 | Latitude_min = 38 | Latitude_sec = 55 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 2 | Longitude_sec = 7 | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Haruna Manyuki Daigongen (榛名満行大権現) | location = {{ill|Numata|qid=Q666317|1=Numata, Gunma}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.64861|N|139.03528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Haruna Shrine''' (榛名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Numata, Gunma|en|Numata, Gunma|qid=Q666317|lt=Numata, Gunma|1=Numata, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The shrine was formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Overview == The exact founding date is unknown. According to records from the {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Shinano Province)|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Shinano Province)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579587}}'s {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|qid=Q11396755}} in {{ill|Sanada, Ueda, Nagano|ja|真田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada, Ueda, Nagano|1=Sanada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583402}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (modern {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|qid=Q844852|lt=Ueda, Nagano|1=Ueda, Nagano}}), a bell inscribed with the name "Haruna Gongen in Usune District, Tonegō, Kōzuke Province" was cast in the 3rd year of {{ill|Shōō (Kamakura period)|en|Shōō (Kamakura period)|qid=Q1327371|lt=Shōō (Kamakura period)|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} (1290), which is believed to be associated with this shrine{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Tone Educational Society|1930|pp=238-240|loc=Part I}}{{Sfn|Numata City Board of Education|1975|pp=243-245}}. According to the ''Numata Jōdai-ki'' (Chronicle of the Numata Castle Lords), in the 1st year of {{ill|Kyōroku|en|Kyōroku|qid=Q1193418|lt=Kyōroku|1=Kyōroku}} (1528), {{ill|Numata Teruyasu|ja|沼田輝泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Teruyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} constructed {{ill|Kurauchijō|ja|倉内城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurauchijō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later known as {{ill|Numata Castle|en|Numata Castle|qid=Q11554573|lt=Numata Castle|1=Numata Castle}}) and relocated the deity of Mt. Hotaka from Kurimori to its current location, where a former {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満宮}} once stood{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Tone Educational Society|1930|pp=238-240|loc=Part I}}. The Haruna deity was initially enshrined in {{ill|Makiwa Castle|ja|幕岩城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiwa Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the residence of {{ill|Numata Akitaka|ja|沼田顕泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Akitaka|1=Numata Akiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554625}}, before being moved to its current site{{Sfn|Numata Town History Editorial Committee|1952|pp=615-617}}{{Sfn|Numata City Board of Education|1975|pp=243-245}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}}. In {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 19 (1734), Haruna Manyuki Daigongen was granted the Rank of {{ill|Shōichii|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 (1869), its name was changed from Haruna Manyuki Daigongen to Haruna Shrine{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}}. The Suwa Shrine was originally located in Takitana (Numata Plateau) and was relocated to Tenzui during the {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1789–1801){{Sfn|Numata Town History Editorial Committee|1952|pp=615-617}}. In April 8, {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 41 (1908), it was merged into Haruna Shrine{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Tone Educational Society|1930|pp=238-240|loc=Part I}}{{Sfn|Numata City Board of Education|1975|pp=243-245}}. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 3 (1928), it was elevated to {{ill|Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} status{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Tone Educational Society|1930|pp=238-240|loc=Part I}}{{Sfn|Numata Town History Editorial Committee|1952|pp=615-617}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}}. In {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 28 (1953), it was incorporated as a {{ill|Religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious corporation|qq=}} under the name Haruna Shrine{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}}. == Shrine Grounds == * '''Stone Torii''' – Erected in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 17 (1640) by {{ill|Sanada Nobumasa|ja|真田信政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada Nobumasa|1=Sanada Nobumasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583366}}, originally belonging to Suwa Shrine before being relocated to this site. * '''Main Hall''' – Built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, with a copper roofing system. According to ''The History of Haruna Shrine as a Prefectural Shrine,'' it was constructed in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 4 (1627) by Sanada Kawachi-no-Kami, and architectural analysis suggests it dates to the early 17th century{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}}. * '''Daikoku-sama''' – A structure originally built as a {{ill|Hōanden|en|Hōanden|qid=Q10942293|lt=Hōan-den|1=Hōanden}} for Numata Elementary School and later repurposed{{Sfn|Numata City Board of Education|1975|pp=243-245}}. * '''Memeyoshi-sama''' – A deity believed to enhance beauty when touched, though its origins are uncertain{{Sfn|Numata City Board of Education|1975|pp=243-245}}. == Annual Festivals == * '''Main Festival''' – August 4 (held during the {{ill|Numata Festival|ja|沼田まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numata Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * '''Suwa Festival''' – 24th day of the 7th lunar month * '''Daikoku Festival''' – October 15 {{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section|2022|pp=235-237}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|lang=ja |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Cultural Heritage Protection Section}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja |title=利根郡誌 |date=1930-08-25 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Tone Educational Society}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja |title=沼田の民俗と伝承 |date=1975-12-15 |publisher=Numata City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja |title=沼田町史沼田を中心とする利根の研究 |date=1952-08-25 |publisher=Numata Town History Editorial Committee}} == External Links == * [http://harunanomori.org/harunajinjya/index.html Haruna Shrine Official Website] {{translated page|ja|榛名神社 (沼田市)|version=102354997|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:榛名神社 (沼田市)]] {{wikidata link|Q131275530}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Numata, Gunma]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Numata, Gunma]] [[Category:Image Requests for Numata City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
